sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After clicking the dark panel at (55, 21), the two bottom panels SWAPPED positions and colors:
- Before: left=blue with yellow shape, right=dark with yellow shape
- After: left=dark with yellow shape, right=blue with yellow shape
The blue border moved from the left panel to the right panel. This means I selected the right panel (which now has the blue border). The dark panel is now on the left.
Also, I notice the target shape in the left panel changed - it's now a dark gray shape at the top. Let me check what changed more carefully.
Now I need to try setting bars and clicking the blue circle to see if the shape moves now that I've selected it.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the target shape position in the left panel
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] > 5 and n['pixels'] < 30:
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
        print(f"Gray shape: center=({cr:.0f}, {cc:.0f}), pixels={n['pixels']}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content: Gray shape: center=(1, 50), pixels=23, children=[]

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Hmm, that's a different shape. Let me look for the target shape in the left panel more carefully. The left panel seems to have a dark shape near the top. Let me check all objects in the left panel region.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check all objects in the left panel region (cols 6-30, rows 1-30)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
    cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
    if cr < 30 and cc < 30 and n['pixels'] > 2:
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, center=({cr:.0f}, {cc:.0f}), pixels={n['pixels']}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

OK so the target shape is at (9, 16) - it's a charcoal (c) shape with 14 pixels, inside the gray (G) background of the left panel. That's the target position for the bottom yellow shape.
Now let me try setting bars and clicking the blue circle to see if the shape moves now that I've selected the correct panel.
But wait - I still don't know which row is UP. Let me try Row 1 first since that's LEFT, and see if anything happens. If the selection matters, maybe now the bars will work.
